title: The Crew: The Quest for Planet Nine

component_identification.content: The Crew includes 40 large task cards (mission objectives), 36 small playing cards numbered 1–9 in four suits/colors, 4 rocket trump cards numbered 1–4, 1 distress signal token, 1 commander token, 1 mission log/rulebook, and optional reminder cards depending on printing. Card counts are fixed and critical for mission setup.

core_game_loop.content: Turn rhythm: active side reveals or frames information, teammates/opponents respond under that game’s communication limits, then resolve points or survival state and rotate responsibility. Teach this as a repeating three-step cycle: present signal, interpret signal, then resolve consequences. New players should always know whose decision matters right now and what hidden information cannot be shared. Most confusion comes from breaking communication restrictions or skipping reveal order, so keep sequence strict.

detailed_rules.content: Deal all cards. Highest rocket (4) is commander and takes commander token. Mission card determines tasks: specific players must win specific cards in tricks. Standard trick-taking applies: must follow suit if able; if void, may play any card including rocket trump. Highest card of led suit wins unless trumped by highest rocket. Communication is tightly limited: each player may place one chosen card face up with token above/middle/below to indicate it is their highest, only, or lowest in that suit (once per mission unless mission text changes). Players then play tricks trying to satisfy all task requirements in legal order. Mission fails immediately when an impossible/forbidden capture occurs; retry same mission. Distress signal and special scenario rules unlock later and can change communication or timing constraints.

scoring_and_endgame.content: No running victory points; each mission is binary success/failure based on task completion. Campaign progress is scoring substitute: clear mission to advance, fail and retry. Endgame is completion of selected mission set or campaign chapter. Some logs track retries as performance metric for group comparison.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Protect task cards early by controlling lead when possible. 2) Save communication token for a moment that prevents mission failure, not trivial information. 3) Count trumps (rockets) and void suits; timing trump control is everything. 4) If mission allows order constraints, plan trick tempo before first lead. 5) On retries, adjust only the failure point—small coordination fixes beat full strategic reset.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 457; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
